title: 15 CRM Fields That Kill Sales Velocity
subtitle: A pipeline health audit for RevOps managers at 10-200 employee B2B companies. Run it against one stage and find the fields that make your forecast lie.

sections.title: Stage Gate Fields — The 5 That Move Deals
sections.description: These are the fields that decide whether a stage change means anything at all.

items.text: Require Exit Criteria Met (Y/N) to advance each stage
items.tip: If a rep can drag a deal to Proposal without checking this, the stage means nothing — enforce with HubSpot required-field logic or Salesforce validation rules

items.text: Populate Next Step Date on every open opportunity
items.tip: Flag anything blank or more than 14 days out — no date = no deal

items.text: Confirm Decision Maker (Y/N) before Proposal stage
items.tip: Attended a call doesn't count — require explicit rep confirmation, not inferred attendance

items.text: Fill Validated Budget Range before pricing goes out
items.tip: Free-text TBD fails the check — use a picklist with USD bands ($0-25k, $25-100k, $100k+)

sections.title: Data Hygiene Fields — The 4 That Kill Forecast Accuracy
sections.description: Stale contact data is the silent forecast killer nobody audits until Q3.

items.text: Refresh email verification status every 7 days, not every 6 weeks
items.tip: Prospeo hits 98% verified accuracy on a 7-day cycle — industry average of 6 weeks is why your sequences bounce

items.text: Verify both phone AND email on the primary contact, not one or the other
items.tip: Single-channel contacts are the #1 source of ghost pipeline

items.text: Auto-enrich employee count, industry, and revenue band — never rep-typed
items.tip: Rep-typed firmographics fail at 30%+ — use Clearbit, Apollo, or ZoomInfo enrichment on contact create

items.text: Run duplicate detection weekly with auto-merge rules for 95%+ matches
items.tip: Manual dedupe never happens — automate it with HubSpot Operations Hub or a scheduled n8n workflow

sections.title: Governance Fields — The 3 That Stop Rep Gaming
sections.description: Without these locks, reps will quietly reshape the pipeline to hit quota.

items.text: Lock stage history from manual edits — zero exceptions
items.tip: Reps should never be able to backdate a stage change — use field-level permissions or audit log enforcement

items.text: Log Close Date changes with a required reason code picklist
items.tip: If a deal slips three times with no reason code, it's not a deal — it's wishful thinking

items.text: Trigger manager approval on Amount changes over 20%
items.tip: Silent pricing drops are how forecasts break mid-quarter — build the approval flow in HubSpot or Salesforce Flow

sections.title: Activity Fields — The 2 That Show Real Progress
sections.description: Opens and clicks don't move deals. These two fields separate real motion from noise.

items.text: Auto-update Last Meaningful Activity only from logged calls, meetings, or replied emails
items.tip: Opens and clicks don't count — this is the single best stale deal filter you can build

items.text: Track Multithreading Count (distinct contacts engaged per deal) at every stage gate
items.tip: Single-threaded deals past Discovery are a forecast risk flag

sections.title: Weekly Scorecard Fields — The 1 That Keeps Everyone Honest
sections.description: Data quality only improves when reps hear about it from their manager, not from RevOps.

items.text: Publish Field Completeness Score per rep weekly, not monthly
items.tip: RevOps owns the flag, managers own the conversation — reps should hear it from their manager using your scorecard

completion_cta.headline: Want the fix for your top 3 gaps?
completion_cta.description: Send me the fields that failed this audit and I'll return the specific enforcement rules — required-field logic, validation rules, and refresh cadence — matched to your CRM. One reply, no call required.

ctas.headline: Your pipeline is leaking deals at the field level. Want it diagnosed, not guessed?
ctas.description: Over 70% of these CRM fields and enforcement rules are missing or broken. At that level, no individual rep fix sticks — the system is selecting against velocity. The Agent-Ready Blueprint is a $2,500 fixed-fee engagement: I review your CRM (HubSpot/Salesforce/Pipedrive), audit field enforcement, validation rules, and refresh cadence, then deliver a 90-day cleanup plan with the exact required-field logic and weekly scorecard your reps need. Budget-defensible whether or not you build with me.

ctas.headline: Real gaps in pipeline hygiene. See the system that enforces them.
ctas.description: 30-70% gaps means your CRM has the right fields but the enforcement is leaky. Tour the Lead Magnet System and Content System to see how I wire required-field gates, validation rules, and refresh cadence into n8n + Claude pipelines for sales teams — plug in the controls your audit flagged.

ctas.headline: Pipeline hygiene is dialed. Stay sharp — here's what comes next.
ctas.description: Under 30% gaps means the field enforcement is mostly in place. The next gains live in stage-by-stage conversion analysis, deal-aging triggers, and forecast accuracy regressions — the kind of work most sales ops never get to. Subscribe to the Agent-Ready Letter — one tactical CRM teardown + one ops case study per week.
